channel.title: Jira
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LS-93] 7.7 Audio Evaluation UI (n6_audio_qa)
item.description:
Epic: LS-84 To Do

Goal: Provide Creators with a rich Quality Assurance (QA) interface to review generated audio sequentially. Crucially, implement Segment-Level Audio Regeneration using Context Padding to fix specific pronunciation or pacing issues without mutating surrounding audio, while perfectly preserving the natural prosody (tone/emotion) of the TTS voice.

Requirements:

  • Language-by-Language QA Flow:
    • Creators review generated audio item by item.
    • They explicitly mark the status of each track as: Approve, Pending, or Comment.
  • Precision Commenting (Timeline & Highlight):
    • Creators mark an exact timestamp or highlight specific text to attach feedback (e.g., "Pronounce this Kanji as X instead of Y").
  • Sentence-Level AI Isolation (guide.audio_fix):
    • The AI analyzes the feedback and identifies the exact single problem sentence.
    • The AI outputs the corrected text for this target sentence, but also identifies its immediate preceding and succeeding sentences to maintain narrative context.
  • Context Padding Generation (Anti-Tone-Shift Strategy):
    • CRITICAL: The AI Orchestrator does not send only the isolated target sentence to the TTS engine (which would cause flat or unnatural tone).
    • Instead, the Orchestrator builds a Padded Text Block: [Previous Sentence] + [Target Sentence (Corrected)] + [Next Sentence].
    • This padded block is sent to the Gemini TTS engine to generate a temporally contiguous audio segment with natural emotional flow.
  • Audio Trimming & Crossfade Stitching (Backend):
    • Using a backend audio processor (e.g., FFmpeg or silence-detection algorithms), the system trims the newly generated padded audio, discarding the audio for the previous and next sentences, keeping only the perfectly intonated target sentence.
    • The system then stitches this new target audio back into the original master timeline.
    • Requirement: The stitch must use a micro-crossfade (e.g., 10-30ms) to prevent any hard "clicking" or unnatural breath pauses at the junction points.

�� Prompt Library — guide.audio_fix

  • System Prompt: You are a meticulous audio engineer. Analyze creator feedback on TTS delivery, identify the exact sentence containing the issue, and apply precise literal text modifications (e.g., phonetic spelling, pause tags) to correct it. You must also output the immediately preceding and succeeding sentences to provide TTS context padding. Output strictly in JSON format.
  • User Prompt Template:
    • Full Script: {{full_script}}
    • Highlighted Text / Issue: {{error_location}}
    • Creator Feedback: {{user_feedback}}
  • Expected Output (Strict JSON):
    • previous_sentence: The exact sentence immediately before the target (empty if at start).
    • target_sentence_original: The exact original sentence with the issue.
    • target_sentence_corrected: The modified sentence with phonetic/tag adjustments.
    • next_sentence: The exact sentence immediately after the target (empty if at end).
    • reasoning: Brief explanation of the mechanical fix.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • [ ] The QA UI presents a clear "Approve", "Pending", and "Comment" decision panel.
  • [ ] The guide.audio_fix prompt successfully isolates the target sentence and accurately captures its contextual neighbors (previous/next).
  • [ ] The Orchestrator successfully sends the 3-sentence "Padded Text Block" to the TTS engine.
  • [ ] The backend audio pipeline successfully trims the padded audio, isolating the target sentence.
  • [ ] The trimmed audio is stitched into the original master audio file using crossfading, resulting in a single seamless .mp3 file without clicks or pops.
  • [ ] The regenerated sentence perfectly resolves the creator's feedback while maintaining the exact emotional prosody and tone of the surrounding audio.
